extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is the conjunction of 10 inputs including polypharmacy. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever polypharmacy is true. Since rec_drug_a=true logically requires polypharmacy=true (it is one of the 10 required conjuncts), the very same input that activates rec_drug_a also activates rec_drug_b. There is no priority conflict because the two recommendations are distinct outputs, each with a single enabling rule, and defaults only apply when no rule fires. Therefore any input satisfying the full conjunction makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. Counterexample: all 10 conjunction inputs true (polypharmacy, anticoagulated, ckd_dialysis, heart_failure, diabetes, immunosuppressed, asthma, g6pd_deficiency, hyperkalemia, low_ejection_fraction), with prior_gi_bleed, elderly, bradycardia set false. This yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously.
